evidence: Each frame centers on the amber probe against the four periwinkle candidates with the lilac score bars as supporting readout; one interaction system carries the focus.
The frame presents a single covariation system (probe, four candidates, four score bars) rather than five-plus unrelated novel ideas at equal weight.
c1..c4 sit at their arrow tips, the probe is labeled on its shaft, and each score bar carries its c1..c4 tag directly beneath it; no legend or key appears.
The only prose is the short instruction 'Drag the probe to score each candidate...' which annotates the visible interactive rather than replacing it.
Candidate labels, the instruction line, and the mono score readouts (e.g. 1.3, -1.0, 0.9) are all legible at each viewport width including 375px.
All arrows, labels, and score bars sit well inside the frame with generous margins at both 1280 and 375 widths.
In length-cheat-1280 the 'c2' candidate label and the 'probe' label abut with no gap, rendering as a merged 'c2probe' string near the top center.
suggestion: Offset the probe label further from the c2 tip label when the probe rests at 70 degrees so the two never touch.

evidence: The probe is amber, candidates periwinkle, score bars lilac, and labels cream throughout; each concept holds one consistent color across states.
Every element—arrows, length handle, score bars, readouts, labels—carries propositional meaning; no mascots or ornaments appear.

Each driven state matches its promise: initial probe near vertical, aligned c1 at 1.0 peak, orthogonal c3 at 1.0 with c1 at 0.0, anti-aligned c1 at -1.0 with bar below baseline, and length-cheat shows c1 (1.3) climbing past c2 (1.0) on length.
Score bars sit some distance below the vector cluster, but each bar's c1..c4 tag keeps the mapping to its arrow unambiguous.
not applicable: frames are independent driven-state snapshots, not a continuous build sequence.
The composition keeps ample negative space and a clear top-to-bottom reading order from instruction to vectors to score bars.
